articles.title: PRICING IS A PRODUCT
articles.summary: Pricing used to be a number you wrote in a spreadsheet, negotiated once a year with the customer, and forgot about until renewal.

articles.title: Keep reading _Pricing is a Product_ on the Metronome blog
BUILD YOUR PERSONAL AI COPILOT
articles.summary: Leveraging AI tools effectively requires providing sufficient context, which turns them into personalized copilots for complex, knowledge-based tasks rather than generic assistants. AI copilots enhance productivity by maintaining consistent awareness of users' goals, enabling sharper strategic decisions and workflow efficiency.

articles.title: NEW FRONTLINES, NEW PRIMES
articles.summary: Defense tech is being reshaped as startups gain traction amid rising global tensions, surging investment, and streamlined government contracting. With legacy players giving way to agile “Neoprimes,” the industry is entering its most transformative era in decades.

articles.title: PRODUCT OPS WITHOUT PERMISSION
articles.summary: A new CPO asking tough questions is your chance to spotlight how product ops can drive strategic execution. Document existing ops work, run a pilot that aligns with leadership priorities, and collaborate with program managers to show the value of formalizing the role without overstepping.

articles.title: WHY YOUR PRODUCT DECISIONS NEED DESIGN EXPERTISE
articles.summary: Companies mistakenly equate business expertise with design skill, creating cluttered products that frustrate users and require excessive documentation. Investing in user-centered design reduces friction, boosts conversions, and lowers support costs.

articles.title: AI IS BLURRING THE LINE BETWEEN PRODUCT AND CX
articles.summary: Join Decagon's product team for a deep dive into the evolving relationship between product and customer experience. Max and Bihan will explore ways to strengthen product-CX collaboration [10], how AI agents are changing customer journeys, and the mindset shift that

articles.title: modern PMs need to make. Join live on August 6
DYNAMIC CONTEXT PROBLEM: HOW CARTA'S INTERNAL AI AGENTS SAVE THOUSANDS OF HOURS OF BACK-AND-FORTH WORK
articles.summary: Carta deployed AI agents to streamline complex fund admin workflows, turning 11-minute tasks into seconds and saving 3,500+ hours monthly. Its success came from deep integration, user collaboration, and rich prompt design that scaled decision-making without sacrificing accuracy.

articles.title: A QUICK PRODUCT SIMPLICITY TEST: REMOVE ALL THE EXPLAINERS
articles.summary: Test product simplicity by removing explainers and NUX. If users can still navigate easily, your design is strong. Relying on NUX often hides design flaws, so focus on making the core experience intuitive.

articles.title: WHERE PRODUCT MEETS FINANCE
articles.summary: Effective collaboration between Product and Finance requires early engagement, precise language, and a clear understanding of financial impacts like revenue recognition and forecasting. Product managers should mirror Finance's structured approach, clarify assumptions collaboratively, and communicate concisely to build trust.

articles.title: THE EVOLUTION OF SOFTWARE PRICING
articles.summary: Software pricing has evolved through six eras, from bundled hardware to hybrid models blending subscriptions with usage. As AI introduces new cost structures, vendors must prioritize simple, transparent pricing tied to customer value to maintain trust and growth.

articles.title: WHY YOUR PRODUCT FEELS BORING AND HOW GAMIFICATION CAN FIX IT
articles.summary: Gamification drives user retention through daily challenges, streaks, leaderboards, contests, and achievements, turning routine interactions into engaging habits.

articles.title: THE JOB IS COMMUNICATION
articles.summary: Effective PMs explicitly state goals, separate facts from opinions, welcome critique, and actively shape their narrative.
